author: @kowalski2385
text: Marco, I trust you've done enough research which will prevent you from personally* investigating  the original visual novel. I myself had heard the name here and there though only in mention. I knew VNs were a strange world full of messed up media. Though, even second hand- I was not prepared for the "cognito-hazard" that is Saya no Uta. So, as a formal warning- do not personally investigate even the censored version of the VN. For it is simultaneously reviled, yet respected...the medium in itself violates the individual almost breaking the 4th wall in a sense. Not in narrative, but in purpose and experience of the very existence of it. YOU, experience a horror, YOU yourself are irrevocably affected by the story, by the game.
video_title: This Gacha Game Has Shockingly Good Music  Ft. Emi Evans

author: @ThatCasThatCas
text: Great character designs, character writing, ost, combat. There is so much to love about this game.
video_title: This Gacha Game Has Shockingly Good Music  Ft. Emi Evans

author: @poppypollen4362
text: Repetition is indeed a point of this song, if you take the narrative of the game and the collab into account. It's all about memory, about not letting go of things so it makes more sense to rotate than meander if you think about it.
video_title: This Gacha Game Has Shockingly Good Music  Ft. Emi Evans
